rationale: Re-pin after the PR #40 edits to tests/acceptance/test_gating.py (module-scope pin; git diff HEAD~2 confirms only test_verdicts_carry_replayable_probes changed -- this test's body is untouched). Spot-check re-executed against the current module: always-all-three-hooks mutant KILLED. Prior review carried forward: all probes via rdm story mutation-probe on rdm/hooks.py, all KILLED. Clauses: (1) the design-gate pre-commit hook installs by default: `DESIGN_GATE_HOOKS = ('pre-commit',)` -> `()` KILLED (default pre-commit must exist and be executable); (2) ONLY that hook by default: `names = DESIGN_GATE_HOOKS + (ISSUE_HOOKS if with_issue_hooks else ())` -> always all three (`DESIGN_GATE_HOOKS + ISSUE_HOOKS`) KILLED by the not-exists assertions on commit-msg / prepare-commit-msg; (3) issue hooks land solely with the explicit flag: same line -> always `DESIGN_GATE_HOOKS` (flag ignored) KILLED by the opt-in X_OK assertions on all three hooks. Executability is asserted (os.access X_OK) for every installed hook.
